steps.description: Opening narrative — set the three pains. Carla rebuilds her distribution plan in Excel every Monday (90 minutes). Devon retypes partner requests from email into a Google Sheet (he calls himself a 'transcription robot'). 15-20% of volunteers no-show, and Devon's Tuesdays are eaten chasing them. Riverside Food Network already pays for NPSP. Today's demo replaces all three on the platform they have.

actions.value: Three problems live on Carla's Monday morning whiteboard: a 90-minute Excel rebuild, Devon retyping partner emails into a Google Sheet, and 15-20% volunteer no-shows. Each one eats staff time. None of them require new software — they require the NPSP you already pay for to do its job.

expected_visible: Riverside Food Network branded title slide or NPSP Home with the three personas named (Carla, Devon, Maria, Jordan; Janet is in the audience)

steps.description: Maria (Centro Latino de Hillsboro) signs into the Riverside Partner Portal (Experience Cloud, Customer Community Plus license) and lands on Partner Home. She sees her current month's allotment: 4,200 lb total, with category breakdown — 1,200 lb produce, 800 lb protein, 1,500 lb dry goods, 700 lb infant formula. Today she would have emailed Devon. Today she does not.
expected_visible: Header reading 'Welcome, Maria Castillo' (Centro Latino de Hillsboro)
Allotment card: '4,200 lb available this month'
Category breakdown tiles: Produce 1,200 lb / Protein 800 lb / Dry Goods 1,500 lb / Infant Formula 700 lb
Primary CTA button labeled 'Request Distribution'

steps.description: Maria clicks 'Request Distribution.' The portal opens a guided form. She selects categories (produce 400 lb, protein 200 lb, infant formula 300 lb), confirms her partner agency address, picks 'Wednesday this week' as the requested distribution date, and clicks Submit. Watch the time stamp — 9:00am Monday. This is the moment Devon's transcription disappears.
expected_visible: Toast message: 'Request submitted — reference PR-00184'
Status row appears in 'My Requests' table: PR-00184 / 900 lb / Wednesday / Status: Submitted
Time-stamp on the new row reads exactly Monday 09:00 AM

steps.description: We switch to Devon's NPSP Lightning Console. His 'Inbound Partner Requests' list view shows PR-00184 already there as a Case (Record Type: Partner_Request, Origin: Portal). No email. No spreadsheet. No retyping. Devon never touched it. This is the step that makes Devon stop calling himself a transcription robot.
expected_visible: List view 'Inbound Partner Requests' with PR-00184 at the top
Case Origin column reads 'Portal' (not 'Email' or 'Phone')
Account column reads 'Centro Latino de Hillsboro'
Created Date column reads Monday 09:00 AM
Devon's queue counter has incremented but he has NOT opened the case — it routed itself

steps.description: Now it is Tuesday morning. We open Carla's Distribution Plan dashboard (NPSP Reports + Dashboards — no Tableau, no new license). The 'This Week's Partner Requests' tile shows PR-00184 already mapped to Truck Route 3 (Hillsboro / Forest Grove loop) and to the Wednesday warehouse pull list. Maria submitted at 9am Monday. By Tuesday morning, no one had to type anything for it to be on Carla's screen, routed.
expected_visible: Dashboard title: 'Distribution Plan — This Week'
Tile 'Partner Requests' shows count 7 (with PR-00184 included)
Tile 'Truck Routes' includes Route 3 — Hillsboro/Forest Grove with PR-00184 (Centro Latino, 900 lb) bound to it
Tile 'Warehouse Pull List' shows Wednesday's pull list including PR-00184 line items (produce 400 / protein 200 / infant formula 300)
Last refreshed timestamp reads Tuesday morning

steps.description: Same portal, different user. Jordan Mendez (volunteer, Customer Community license) logs into the Volunteer side of the Experience Cloud site. Her home shows three upcoming shifts. Today she would have emailed Devon to swap. Today she does not.
expected_visible: Header reading 'Welcome, Jordan Mendez'
Three upcoming shifts: Saturday 9am Mobile Distribution / Tuesday 2pm Warehouse Sort / Thursday 5pm Bethany Pantry
Each shift card has 'Reschedule' and 'Cancel' actions inline

steps.description: Jordan clicks Reschedule on her Saturday shift, picks the next-week Saturday 9am slot, and confirms. The change is live. No email. No Devon. The portal updates her shift inline and the originating shift slot reopens for someone else.
expected_visible: Toast: 'Shift rescheduled — confirmation sent'
My Shifts list now shows Saturday next week (instead of this Saturday)
Original Saturday this-week slot returned to the open shifts list (visible in the inline 'Open shifts' band)

steps.description: Now back to Devon. A different volunteer no-showed for Thursday's mobile distribution at Bethany Pantry. In the old world, Devon hunts a printed sign-in sheet on Tuesday and starts emailing. In the new world, an open Case is already in his queue — Subject 'No-show: Marcus Halloran — Thursday 5pm Bethany' — auto-created the moment the shift end-time passed without a check-in.
expected_visible: List view 'Volunteer No-Shows' with one new row
Subject column: 'No-show: Marcus Halloran — Thursday 5pm Bethany'
Origin: 'Auto-Created'
Status: 'New'
Owner: 'Devon Park'

steps.description: Devon opens the no-show Case. The detail page shows the volunteer's name, the missed shift, the volunteer's history (third no-show this quarter), and a pre-populated follow-up email template that Devon can send in two clicks. What used to be a 3-hour Tuesday spreadsheet hunt is now a 4-click triage. Devon gets his Tuesdays back.
expected_visible: Case detail page: Subject 'No-show: Marcus Halloran — Thursday 5pm Bethany'
Related Contact: Marcus Halloran (Volunteer)
Related Shift: Thursday 5pm Bethany Pantry Mobile Distribution
Volunteer history component shows 'Third no-show this quarter'
Email tab pre-populated with 'Volunteer Follow-up' template, Send button visible

steps.description: Now Carla. Monday morning. We open her Distribution Plan dashboard cold — no rebuild. The Protein tile is red: she is 600 lb short of what Thursday's distributions need. She sees this in 3 seconds, not 90 minutes. This is the screen that replaces her Excel rebuild entirely.
expected_visible: Dashboard title 'Distribution Plan — This Week'
Protein tile rendered red, showing '600 lb short of Thursday demand'
Produce / Dry Goods / Infant Formula tiles rendered green
'Open Partner Requests' counter: 7 (PR-00184 visible in the drill-down list)
'Unfilled Volunteer Slots' counter: 4
Route map tile rendered, showing 5 active routes for the week

steps.description: Carla clicks the 'Unfilled Volunteer Slots' tile and the route map tile. The slot tile drills into the 4 open shifts (including the one Jordan vacated earlier). The route map tile expands to show truck assignments — Route 3 already has Centro Latino's request bound to it from step 5. All three streams (partner requests, volunteer slots, routing) are on one screen.
expected_visible: Drill-down list shows 4 open shifts including 'Saturday 9am Mobile Distribution' (the one Jordan vacated)
Route map drill-down lists Route 3 — Hillsboro/Forest Grove — Wed — Centro Latino de Hillsboro (PR-00184)
Both drill-downs render in the same dashboard view, no page refresh

steps.description: Carla clicks the red Protein tile. It drills into the partner requests still expecting protein this week. She sees that one of them (Bethany Hills Family Pantry, 250 lb) can be slid to next week without missing their allotment window. She inline-edits the 'Distribution Date' field on that Case to next Wednesday and saves. The Protein tile drops from 600 lb short to 350 lb short — green-on-the-margin instead of red. Carla closes the laptop. Total time on the dashboard: under 4 minutes. Compare with last week's 90-minute Excel rebuild.
expected_visible: Drill-down shows 'Partner Requests — Protein' with 4 requests
Bethany Hills Family Pantry record opens inline; Requested_Date field editable
After save, Protein tile re-renders showing '350 lb short of Thursday demand' in amber, not red
Toast message confirms 'Bethany Hills request rescheduled to next Wednesday'
Dashboard timestamp updates to current time
